commands.review_summary:
The PR changes ClawSweeper PR status routing so security reviews marked needs_attention can route to ready for maintainer look when the recommended merge-risk option is accept_risk, and adds focused status-label tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main's hasUnresolvedContributorWork treats every securityReview.status === needs_attention as unresolved contributor work, and prStatusLabelKind maps that to waiting_on_author before ready_for_maintainer_look.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof, not a code repair ClawSweeper can make on the branch.

target.title: Fix PR status routing for security owner review
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #263.

This changes PR status label routing so `securityReview.status: needs_attention` is not always treated as contributor-facing work.

When the durable review has:

- sufficient/override/not-applicable proof,
- a correct patch,
- no P0-P2 review findings, and
- a recommended merge-risk option of `accept_risk`,

ClawSweeper now routes the PR to the existing `status: 👀 ready for maintainer look` label instead of `status: ⏳ waiting on author`.

Security reviews that recommend `fix_before_merge`, or security reviews with no structured recommended `accept_risk` option, still route as unresolved contributor work. That preserves the safer default for real author-remediable security defects.

## Validation

- `pnpm run build:all`
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "ClawSweeper PR status" test/clawsweeper.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/clawsweeper.ts test/clawsweeper.test.ts`
- `pnpm run check:active-surface`
- `pnpm run check:limits`
- `pnpm run lint:src`
- `pnpm run lint:scripts`

I also attempted the full `node --test test/clawsweeper.test.ts` locally, but this Windows desktop environment hits unrelated existing subprocess assumptions around Codex proof checks and `/usr/bin/git`. The focused status-label tests pass after `build:all`.
target.author: brokemac79
